name: bash
description: Execute a bash command in the shell. Use this to run any command-line operation.

name: read_file
description: Read the contents of a file at the given path.

name: write_file
description: Write content to a file at the given path. Creates the file if it does not exist, overwrites if it does.

path.description: The file path to write to

content.description: The content to write to the file

name: create_task
description: Create a new task definition. Validates the input and writes a JSON task file to the tasks directory. The task can then be run via CLI or scheduled.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this task does

prompt.description: The instruction prompt the agent will receive when running this task

schedule.description: How often to run (e.g. '30m', '1h', '24h'). Required if run_mode is 'scheduled'

model.description: Model override for this task (optional, empty uses agent default)

memory_dir.description: Directory for persistent memory across runs (optional)

register_scheduled.description: If 'true' and task is scheduled, register it with the running scheduler immediately

overwrite.description: If 'true', overwrite an existing task with the same name instead of erroring

skills.description: JSON object mapping skill name to file path (e.g. {"api-patterns": "skills/api-patterns.md"})

allowed_tools.description: JSON array of tool names this task is allowed to use (e.g. ["bash", "read_file"])

max_retries.description: Maximum number of retry attempts on failure (e.g. '3')

name: save_script
description: Save a reusable script to the scripts directory. Sets executable permissions. Scripts can be invoked later via the bash tool.

description.description: What the script does (saved as a comment header)

name: save_skill
description: Save a reusable skill (markdown knowledge file) to the skills directory. Skills are injected into the system prompt when a task references them, providing learned knowledge for future runs.

content.description: Skill content in markdown format — patterns, procedures, or knowledge learned during task execution

name: test_task
description: Test-run a task through the real execution pipeline. Loads the task from disk, runs it with a fresh agent, and returns the result (PASSED/FAILED with output and token usage). Use this after creating a task to verify it works before finalizing.

task_name.description: The name of the task to test (must exist in the tasks directory)
